groups.name: Friends of the Chicago River

groups.name: Friends of the Parks
groups.description: Founded 1975. Community voice for Chicago's 600+ parks. Advocacy, stewardship, and volunteerism for equitable park access. New Executive Director Brian Gladstein appointed March 2025 for 50th anniversary.
groups.verification_evidence: WTTW Mar 12 2025: new executive director. WBEZ Apr 15 2025 interview. 50th anniversary. Active advocacy programming.

groups.name: Openlands
groups.description: Founded 1963. Protects natural and open spaces across northeastern Illinois. TreeKeepers program for citizen arborists, tree planting events, community garden support, conservation advocacy.
groups.verification_evidence: Website openlands.org with active CERVIS volunteer registration system. Facebook 10K likes, 134 talking. TreeKeepers program ongoing.

groups.name: Southwest Organizing Project (SWOP)
groups.description: Broad-based organization of Christian, Muslim, and Jewish faith institutions, schools, and community organizations. Affordable housing development, community organizing, youth leadership. Reclaiming Southwest Chicago campaign.
groups.verification_evidence: April 2026 groundbreaking for 44-unit affordable housing. March 2026 $233K FHLBank grant. 2025 Run the Lawn 5K. Chicago Cares volunteer events. Facebook active.

groups.name: Southeast Environmental Task Force (SETF)
groups.description: Nonprofit serving the Southeast Side and south suburbs since 1989. Environmental education, pollution prevention, sustainable development. Renovated ADA-compliant office in Hegewisch.
groups.verification_evidence: Borderless Magazine Jan 27 2026: SETF office in Hegewisch confirmed active, storing EJ materials and PPE. Senator Duckworth visited renovated space. Facebook 3,072 likes.

groups.name: Alliance of the Southeast (ASE)
groups.description: Multicultural, interfaith coalition of churches, schools, businesses, and community organizations. Youth leadership, tenant organizing, anti-violence campaigns, community development advocacy.
groups.verification_evidence: Website asechicago.org live with active events page. Instagram 995 followers, 133 posts. Idealist profile. UChicago Civic Engagement partner.

groups.name: Greater Ashburn Development Association (GADA)
groups.description: 501(c)(3) community development association. Traffic safety planning, neighborhood revitalization, housing and corridor redevelopment. Partnered with CMAP on Safety Action Plan adopted Sept 2025.
groups.verification_evidence: Facebook post May 16 2025. Safety Action Plan adopted Sept 2025 with CMAP. Google Sites page live. Southwest Chicago Post coverage March 2025.

groups.name: TCA Health
groups.description: Federally Qualified Health Center established 1970 in Altgeld Gardens. Primary care, dental, behavioral health. 7,300+ patients annually. Nutrition and Wellness Center under construction with food pantry and VeggieRx program.
groups.verification_evidence: Website tcahealth.org live. Yelp reviews Sept 2025 confirm active operations. Jan 2025 hiring event. Nutrition center groundbreaking Nov 2024.

groups.name: Metropolitan Family Services
groups.description: Founded 1857. Calumet Center serves Altgeld Gardens families. Family Works program provides employment, education, youth, clinical, and senior services. $150K grant to reopen Altgeld food pantry 2025-2026.
groups.verification_evidence: $150,000 Chicago Community Impact Grant 2025-2026 to reopen Altgeld food pantry. LinkedIn post confirmed. Website metrofamily.org live.

groups.name: Howard Brown Health
groups.description: Founded 1974. One of the nation's largest LGBTQ+ organizations. Primary care, STI testing, HIV/AIDS services, TGNC healthcare, behavioral health across multiple Chicago clinics. Serves 40,000+ annually.
groups.verification_evidence: Windy City Times April 17 2026: volunteer celebration event. Website live with active volunteer portal (Better Impact). Multiple clinic locations.

groups.name: Chicago House
groups.description: Supports individuals impacted by HIV/AIDS and the LGBTQ+ community. Housing, health, employment, and TransLife Care program. Serves 3,000+ annually. 2025 strategic vision through 2030.
groups.verification_evidence: Website chicagohouse.org live on Squarespace. 2025 new strategic vision. TransLife Care program with drop-in centers. Serves 3,000+ annually.

groups.name: Illinois Worker Cooperative Alliance (IWCA)
groups.description: Formed 2015. Supports worker cooperatives for low-wage workers and the working poor. Co-op education, technical assistance, ecosystem building. Co-founded by Centro de Trabajadores Unidos.
groups.verification_evidence: Website iwca.coop live on Wix. 2025 annual report published March 2026. Seed Commons partner. Listed in Chicago Workers' Rights annual report.

groups.name: Little Brothers - Friends of the Elderly (LBFE) Chicago
groups.description: National network since 1959. Relieves isolation and loneliness among elderly through volunteer visits, social events, and intergenerational connections. Chicago chapter active.
groups.verification_evidence: Website lbfechicago.org live with active Tribe Events calendar. WordPress site with volunteer and donation pages.

groups.name: The Night Ministry
groups.description: Provides human connection, housing support, and healthcare to unhoused Chicagoans. Street medicine outreach, CTA outreach, youth housing programs, health outreach bus. Participant in 2025 Point-in-Time Count.
groups.verification_evidence: Website thenightministry.org live. Jan 15 2026 volunteer program update. Multiple active job postings 2025-2026. 2025 PIT Count participant.

groups.name: Community Kitchen Chicago
groups.description: Food service program providing free meals to in-need residents, families, and workers. 145,000+ meals served. Maintains Chicago Area Mutual Aid Map and Directory.
groups.verification_evidence: Squarespace website live. Oct 2025 Mutual Aid Map PDF distributed through After School Matters. Chicago Reader Best of Chicago nomination. Linktree active.
